title: Session history persistence
why_it_matters: By default, Codex CLI saves every session transcript — your prompts and the commands run — in plaintext to history.jsonl under CODEX_HOME, with no expiry. Anything sensitive you type or paste is retained on disk until you delete it yourself.

outcomes.when: history.persistence is unset (documented default "save-all" applies) or explicitly "save-all"
outcomes.message: Codex CLI persists full session history to disk in plaintext with no expiry.

outcomes.when: history.persistence is set to a value outside the documented enum
outcomes.message: history.persistence is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: Value is outside the documented enum for the tested version range; it cannot be interpreted safely.

sources.title: Codex configuration reference
sources.notes: Documents [history] persistence values save-all | none. The developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference alias redirects to this canonical URL.

sources.title: Codex advanced configuration
sources.notes: States the default persistence behavior (save-all) applied when the key is unset. The developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced alias redirects to this canonical URL.

limitations: Project-level config is not inspected in this slice; only the user-scope config.toml is read.
This rule cannot confirm any server-side or remote retention — no local file can.
Auth method (ChatGPT sign-in vs API key) changes data-policy interpretation and is user-confirmed-or-unknown; this rule never infers it.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
history.persistence set to a value outside allowed_render (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
